title: Feat + Toolbars: Toolbar button groups
rationale: Facts: Team member E Nelson (elnelson575) requests a toolbar button group wrapper — a minimal container that works for normal-size buttons standalone and inside a toolbar context. Body is brief; no design details or examples beyond the toolbar_button_group() / toolbar_input_button() sketch. Classification hypothesis: Feature request / internal team planning issue for the bslib toolbar component. Duplicate search: Toolbar-related open issues include #1316 (toolbar_badge), #1317 (align=center), and prior toolbar work (#1247, #1249, #1257, #1292); none cover a button group wrapper. No duplicate found. Wrong-location check: bslib owns toolbar layout components; correct repo. Regression evidence: N/A — new feature. Reproduction plan: N/A — enhancement. Impact: Low — narrow toolbar enhancement; no user breakage; clearly scoped team planning item. Priority: Low. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Low; route to milestone planning.

title: feat: Port Skills from posit-dev/skills
rationale: Facts: Team member E Nelson (elnelson575) requests porting bslib Claude Code skills from posit-dev/skills into the bslib repo, and optionally adding a GitHub Action to keep them in sync. Body is brief. Classification hypothesis: Internal developer-tooling enhancement — shipping AI coding skills alongside the package. Duplicate search: No prior bslib issue found about bundling skills. Sister issue py-shiny #2268 has the same request for py-shiny; these are distinct issues in distinct repos, not duplicates of each other. Wrong-location check: Skills that teach bslib-specific patterns belong in the bslib repo; correct location. Regression evidence: N/A. Reproduction plan: N/A. Impact: Low — improves AI-assisted developer experience; does not affect end users or runtime behavior. Priority: Low. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Low; coordinate with posit-dev/py-shiny #2268 if a cross-repo sync pattern is desired.

title: feat: Add test mode with export_test_values() and /dataobj/shinytest snapshot endpoint
rationale: Facts: Core contributor Barret Schloerke (schloerke) files a detailed Phase A engineering spec for porting R Shiny's test mode to py-shiny. Scope includes: enabling via SHINY_TESTMODE=1 env var, export_test_values() module-level function and session method, per-session /session/<token>/dataobj/shinytest snapshot endpoint returning {input, output, export}, best-effort JSON serialization with a tagged error marker, and session.get_test_snapshot_url() helper. Out-of-scope items explicitly deferred to Phase B. References related issues #1156 (broader testing API), #1281 (Playwright controller epic), #1980 (save/restore state). Classification hypothesis: Feature request / internal engineering spec — Phase A of a multi-phase testing story. Duplicate search: #1156 is a broader testing namespace request; this spec is a concrete subset. #1281 is the Playwright controller epic; this is the server-side prerequisite. No duplicate. Wrong-location check: py-shiny owns session routing, input serialization, and reactive context; correct repo. Regression evidence: N/A. Reproduction plan: N/A (spec, not bug). Impact: Medium — enables future snapshot-testing tooling for py-shiny apps; important testing infrastructure milestone; does not affect current end users. Priority: Medium — well-defined, moderate-impact infrastructure feature from a core contributor. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Medium; track alongside #1281 (Playwright epic).

title: [Feature]: Ship py-shiny skills with py-shiny
rationale: Facts: Team member E Nelson (elnelson575) requests that an up-to-date Claude Code skill be shipped with py-shiny. Body is brief: 'Make the skill. Add the skill.' Issue already carries labels 'enhancement' and 'needs-triage'. Classification hypothesis: Internal developer-tooling enhancement — bundling AI coding skills alongside the package for better AI-assisted developer experience. Duplicate search: No prior py-shiny issue for bundled skills. Sister issue bslib #1319 has the same request for bslib; distinct issue in a different repo. Wrong-location check: Skills teaching py-shiny patterns belong in py-shiny; correct repo. Regression evidence: N/A. Reproduction plan: N/A. Impact: Low — benefits AI-assisted developers; zero runtime impact on end users. Priority: Low. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Low, remove needs-triage.

title: Focus is dropped after cancelling a streaming response
rationale: Facts: Maintainer Carson Sievert (cpsievert) reports that clicking the Stop Generating button causes focus to move onto that button, then the button transitions to a spinner and back to a disabled send button, dropping focus to <body>. Expected behavior: focus moves to the textarea so the user can type a follow-up immediately. Suggested fix: call focus on textarea inside cancelStream in js/src/chat/ChatContainer.tsx around line 134. Author notes this is pre-existing behavior unrelated to an in-progress uploads branch. Classification hypothesis: UX bug — focus management error in the cancel-stream path. Duplicate search: No prior shinychat issue found for focus loss on stream cancel. Wrong-location check: shinychat owns ChatContainer.tsx and the cancel-stream UI; correct repo. Regression evidence: Author calls this 'pre-existing behavior' — no specific version regression cited; insufficient evidence to label regression. Reproduction plan: Open a shinychat app, start a streaming response, click Stop Generating, observe that focus drops to <body> rather than the textarea. Impact: Affects all users who cancel a stream and want to type a follow-up; workaround is trivially clicking the textarea. Fix is a 1-line change already identified. Priority: Low — UX papercut with a trivial workaround; does not affect core functionality. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Low; small fix candidate for next release.

title: Add User Icon or Avatar for shinychat
rationale: Facts: External user jlxAtNovozymes (jlxAtNovozymes, affiliation Novozymes) requests user avatar/icon support so each user's messages show their profile image in a multi-user shinychat app. Created 2026-01-14; recently updated 2026-06-03 when maintainer cpsievert posted a detailed workaround comment: assistant icon is already supported via icon_assistant in chat_ui(); user-message avatars can be added today with CSS targeting .shiny-chat-user-message and a session-injected CSS variable containing the avatar URL. No dedicated icon_user parameter exists yet. Classification hypothesis: Feature request — add a first-class icon_user / icon parameter to chat_ui() for user-side message avatars. Duplicate search: No prior shinychat issue for per-user avatar support. Wrong-location check: shinychat owns chat_ui() and message rendering; correct repo. Regression evidence: N/A. Reproduction plan: N/A (feature request). Impact: Low — nice-to-have branding/UX feature for enterprise multi-user apps; functional CSS workaround documented by maintainer in the issue. Priority: Low — clear workaround exists; niche production use case. Recommended next action: Label Priority: Low; consider for UX polish milestone.
